matches.moment: Austria's right-back Posch is caught high in the 68th minute; Yamal exploits the channel and finishes to seal Spain's clean sheet.
Leão's run in behind Croatia's high line at 61', set up by Fernandes' lofted through ball after Croatia commit numbers forward, stretches the defensive block and results in a composed finish.
Mahrez ties the match at 57' via a set-piece driven dribble sequence; Switzerland regain the lead through a penalty won by Embolo after Algeria's backline over-commits to stopping Manzambi's run.

verdict: Three Round of 32 matches today consolidate the narrative of this expanded 48-team World Cup: the favorites from Europe's elite have cleared the group stage without drama, and the knockout phase now sorts them against the ambitious qualifiers who survived the opening gauntlet on minimum margins. Spain are the cleanest story in the tournament — 0 goals conceded, 2.00 xG per game generated, a settled system without over-dependence on any single player. Austria had enough in them to reach this point but their 0% clean sheet rate and defensive exposure vs Argentina signals that Rangnick's side have hit the ceiling of what their personnel can sustain. Portugal enter their most uncertain fixture: Colombia outxG'd the Selecao 1.70 to 0.90 in matchday 3, and Ronaldo's blank sheet against organized defenses is a legitimate concern. Croatia's knockout pedigree means Dalic will manufacture a defensive structure that Portugal must actively break down. Switzerland vs Algeria is the day's most legitimate 50/50 contest, with Switzerland's fresh legs (8 days' rest), Manzambi's electric form, and Algeria's historically porous defense pointing narrowly to the Swiss advancing — but Mahrez remains a tournament-altering individual who will extract at least one chance and likely convert it, making the final score tighter than the rankings suggest. — The Analyst.

players.name: Lamine Yamal
players.stat: 1 goal, 1 assist this WC; 17 years old; most direct dribbler in the tournament
players.watch: Whether he exploits Austria's exposed right flank (Posch) at the same level as vs Saudi Arabia — if fully unleashed, the Austrian right side cannot contain him for 90 minutes

players.name: Mikel Oyarzabal
players.stat: 2 goals this WC (both vs Saudi Arabia); clinical penalty-box finisher who loses markers with late runs
players.watch: Late arrivals into the Austrian box from depth — his conversion in tight spaces is his signature and Austria have not kept a clean sheet in three attempts

players.name: Cristiano Ronaldo
players.stat: 2 goals this WC (first player to score at 6 World Cups; 144th and 145th international goals)
players.watch: Timing of late runs against Croatia's high defensive line on Fernandes' lofted through balls — his positioning in the box remains elite even at 41

players.name: Johann Manzambi
players.stat: 3 goals this WC — FIFA Young Player of Tournament candidate; volley vs Bosnia was standout goal of the group stage
players.watch: Second-post arrivals and cuts across the penalty spot; he has been in the right place with alarming consistency and Algeria's central defenders are susceptible to runs that drag them wide

players.name: Riyad Mahrez
players.stat: 2 goals this WC (both vs Austria, 60' and 90+3'); 35 years old, still a match-winner from set pieces and dribbling sequences
players.watch: Drifting centrally from the right to thread passes into Gouiri or shoot on his stronger foot — Switzerland's left side (Rodriguez) is his primary target zone
